factors.summary: Order backlog or book-to-bill is growing, with future contracted revenues increasing and a significant portion expected to be recognized in the next twelve months.

evidence.text: We consider total company future contracted revenues a forward-looking performance indicator. As of March 31, 2021, we had approximately $1.79 billion of total company future contracted revenue, which included both recognized contract liabilities as well as amounts that will be invoiced and recognized in future periods. We expect to recognize between 20% - 25% of this balance over the next twelve months, and expect the remainder to be recognized over the following five to seven years, subject to risks related to delayed deployments, budget appropriation or other contract cancellation clauses.

factors.summary: The company is successfully raising prices, particularly for newer TASER devices, which has contributed to revenue growth.

evidence.text: Revenue was also impacted by higher average selling prices for TASER 7 and X26P. Revenue from consumer TASER Pulse devices increased due to a substantial increase in volume, partially offset by lower average selling prices.

factors.summary: The Software and Sensors segment is growing fastest, driven by increased user adoption and sales of Axon Body cameras and cloud services.

evidence.text: Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment increased 34.7% during the three months ended March 31, 2021 as we continued to add users and associated devices to our network. The increase in the aggregate number of users resulted in increased Axon Evidence revenue of $13.1 million. Sales of our Axon Body 3 camera drove most of the $6.9 million increase in Axon Body revenue and the increase of $2.0 million in Axon Dock revenue.

factors.summary: Input costs are stable, with the company managing supply chain constraints through diversification and strategic relationships.

evidence.text: Although we have experienced port constraints and some increases in raw materials costs, we have remained focused on closely monitoring our supply chain, and mitigating impacts to keep our gross margins predictable and our inventory steady. For instance, during the recent global slowdown in manufacturing affected by the blockage in the Suez Canal, we duplicated orders by ocean and air, and worked with suppliers to hold higher buffers for Axon on their shelves. We have also worked to mitigate raw materials costs increases through supplier negotiations and buying added non-expiring raw materials.

factors.summary: Labor costs are manageable, with the company investing in headcount to support growth, particularly in research and development.

evidence.text: Salaries, benefits and bonus expense increased $9.5 million primarily due to an increase in headcount. Included in this increase was $1.4 million in payroll taxes related to the vesting of the first tranche of our XSPP in March 2021. Sales and marketing expenses increased $3.3 million, driven by a $2.4 million increase in commissions expense tied to higher revenues and by higher spending on content development, promotional videos, and advertising. Professional, consulting and lobbying expenses decreased $4.3 million, primarily driven by lower expenses related to the FTC litigation.

factors.summary: Gross margins are improving, particularly in the TASER segment, due to fixed cost leverage, manufacturing improvements, and favorable product mix.

evidence.text: Gross margin for the TASER segment increased to 66.7% from 60.1% for the three months ended March 31, 2021 and 2020, respectively. The increase was a result of fixed cost leverage, manufacturing cost improvement, and product mix, as discussed above. As a percentage of net sales, gross margin for the Software and Sensors segment decreased to 59.7% from 60.3% for the three months ended March 31, 2021 and 2020, respectively.

factors.summary: Cost reduction programs are delivering measurable savings, with manufacturing improvements expected to generate significant annual savings.

evidence.text: We are building manufacturing capacity to support our TASER device and cartridge manufacturing lines in response to growing international and federal demand and an increased install base. Investments in manufacturing capacity so far in 2021 have resulted in an approximately 40% capacity increase in TASER 7 propulsion module and cartridge line production capacity, combined with greater per-person efficiency that will generate $1 million in gross cost annual run rate savings on the TASER 7. Manufacturing and supply chain improvements are on track to result in more than $4 million in gross costs savings in 2021.

factors.summary: Productivity is improving due to manufacturing efficiency gains and better utilization of fixed costs.

evidence.text: Approximately half of the improvement was attributable to a combination of manufacturing cost improvement and strong demand for our premium TASER offerings, which resulted in a favorable product mix. The remaining improvement was attributable to additional leverage on fixed costs.

factors.summary: The supply chain is stable with the company taking proactive measures to mitigate disruptions, such as diversifying shipping routes and maintaining higher inventory buffers.

evidence.text: We have remained focused on closely monitoring our supply chain, and mitigating impacts to keep our gross margins predictable and our inventory steady. For instance, during the recent global slowdown in manufacturing affected by the blockage in the Suez Canal, we duplicated orders by ocean and air, and worked with suppliers to hold higher buffers for Axon on their shelves.

factors.summary: Manufacturing capacity utilization is increasing, with investments in production lines leading to higher capacity and efficiency.

evidence.text: We are building manufacturing capacity to support our TASER device and cartridge manufacturing lines in response to growing international and federal demand and an increased install base. Investments in manufacturing capacity so far in 2021 have resulted in an approximately 40% capacity increase in TASER 7 propulsion module and cartridge line production capacity, combined with greater per-person efficiency that will generate $1 million in gross cost annual run rate savings on the TASER 7.

factors.summary: Inventory levels are steady, with the company managing supply chain constraints through strategic measures to avoid shortages or excess.

evidence.text: We have remained focused on closely monitoring our supply chain, and mitigating impacts to keep our gross margins predictable and our inventory steady. For instance, during the recent global slowdown in manufacturing affected by the blockage in the Suez Canal, we duplicated orders by ocean and air, and worked with suppliers to hold higher buffers for Axon on their shelves.

factors.summary: Labor relations are stable, with no notable issues or disruptions mentioned in the text.

evidence.text: No specific information on labor relations or potential disputes was provided in the text.

factors.summary: The company is successfully attracting and retaining talent, with headcount increasing and investments in employee compensation and benefits.

evidence.text: Salaries, benefits and bonus expense increased $9.5 million primarily due to an increase in headcount. Included in this increase was $1.4 million in payroll taxes related to the vesting of the first tranche of our XSPP in March 2021.

factors.summary: The company is facing regulatory changes, specifically related to the FTC litigation, but the impact on operations is minimal.

evidence.text: As discussed in Note 11 of the notes to our condensed consolidated financial statements within this Report on Form 10-Q, we sued the FTC in the District of Arizona, and the FTC filed an enforcement action regarding our May 2018 acquisition of Vievu LLC. Expenses related to the matter were minimal during the three months ended March 31, 2021.

factors.summary: Near-term risks include potential supply chain disruptions and geopolitical factors, but the company is taking measures to mitigate these risks.

evidence.text: We have remained focused on closely monitoring our supply chain, and mitigating impacts to keep our gross margins predictable and our inventory steady. For instance, during the recent global slowdown in manufacturing affected by the blockage in the Suez Canal, we duplicated orders by ocean and air, and worked with suppliers to hold higher buffers for Axon on their shelves. We continue to bolster our strategic relationships in our supply chain, work to identify secondary sourcing, and build in shipping and inventory buffers, which has kept our supply chain execution solid.

factors.summary: The company's long-term strategic direction is clear, focusing on expanding the Software and Sensors segment and investing in cloud-connected devices and AI technologies.

evidence.text: We are investing in technologies that include our conducted energy devices, body cameras, in-car cameras and other sensors, artificial intelligence, digital evidence management, productivity software, communications software, and technologies that enable real-time situational awareness for public safety.

factors.summary: The company faces challenges due to the ongoing impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on the global economy, which has led to an economic downturn. However, the company is adapting through strategic initiatives to address these economic conditions.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic has adversely affected workforces, economies, and financial markets globally, leading to an economic downturn.

As an essential provider of products and services for law enforcement and other first responders, we remain focused on protecting the health and wellbeing of our employees while assuring the continuity of our business operations.

factors.summary: The company faces key sensitivities such as raw material costs, labor costs, and supply chain disruptions, which management is actively monitoring and attempting to mitigate. Despite challenges like port constraints and increased raw material costs, the company has implemented strategies to maintain steady inventory and predictable gross margins.

evidence.text: None.

Although we have experienced port constraints and some increases in raw materials costs, we have remained focused on closely monitoring our supply chain, and mitigating impacts to keep our gross margins predictable and our inventory steady. For instance, during the recent global slowdown in manufacturing affected by the blockage in the Suez Canal, we duplicated orders by ocean and air, and worked with suppliers to hold higher buffers for Axon on their shelves. We have also worked to mitigate raw materials costs increases through supplier negotiations and buying added non-expiring raw materials.

factors.summary: The company has mixed catalysts. While the Software and Sensors segment shows strong growth driven by user adoption and new product launches, there is no mention of specific near-term catalysts in the Other Information section.

evidence.text: None.

Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment increased 34.7% during the three months ended March 31, 2021 as we continued to add users and associated devices to our network. The increase in the aggregate number of users resulted in increased Axon Evidence revenue of $13.1 million. Sales of our Axon Body 3 camera drove most of the $6.9 million increase in Axon Body revenue and the increase of $2.0 million in Axon Dock revenue. The increase in the aggregate number of users and devices also resulted in increased extended warranty revenues of $2.0 million.

factors.summary: The company has mixed forward guidance. While management provided specific revenue and capital expenditure expectations for 2021, they did not offer any forward guidance in the risk_factors section.

evidence.text: None.

For the year ending December 31, 2021, we expect revenue in the range of $780 million to $820 million. Our expectation for capital expenditures of approximately $65 million to $70 million in 2021 remains unchanged.

We consider total company future contracted revenues a forward-looking performance indicator. As of March 31, 2021, we had approximately $1.79 billion of total company future contracted revenue, which included both recognized contract liabilities as well as amounts that will be invoiced and recognized in future periods. We expect to recognize between 20% - 25% of this balance over the next twelve months, and expect the remainder to be recognized over the following five to seven years, subject to risks related to delayed deployments, budget appropriation or other contract cancellation clauses.

factors.summary: Demand trends are positive, with acceleration in premium TASER offerings and growth in the TASER segment driven by increased sales of newer devices and higher average selling prices.

evidence.text: Reflecting strong demand for our premium TASER offerings, manufacturing cost improvement, and fixed cost leverage.

Net sales for the TASER segment increased 30.4% primarily due to a net increase of $17.3 million in TASER device sales and an increase of $3.8 million in cartridge revenue. We continue to see a shift to purchases of our latest generation device, TASER 7, from legacy devices. Revenue was also impacted by higher average selling prices for TASER 7 and X26P.

factors.summary: Unit volumes are growing across the board, driven by strong demand for premium TASER offerings and significant increases in newer devices like TASER 7 and cartridge sales.

evidence.text: Reflecting strong demand for our premium TASER offerings, manufacturing cost improvement, and fixed cost leverage.
Net unit sales for TASER segment products and Software and Sensors segment products were as follows: TASER 7: 23,360 units (104.4% increase), Cartridges: 1,009,760 units (15.6% increase).

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is accelerating across the company, with significant increases in both the TASER and Software and Sensors segments. The growth is driven by increased sales of TASER devices and cartridges, as well as a rise in users and associated devices in the Software and Sensors segment.

evidence.text: Our revenues for the three months ended March 31, 2021 were $195.0 million, an increase of $47.9 million, or 32.5%, from the comparable period in the prior year.
Net sales for the TASER segment increased 30.4% primarily due to a net increase of $17.3 million in TASER device sales and an increase of $3.8 million in cartridge revenue.
Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment increased 34.7% during the three months ended March 31, 2021 as we continued to add users and associated devices to our network.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is shifting positively towards higher-margin products and segments, particularly in the Software and Sensors segment, driven by increased sales of premium offerings like Axon Body cameras and cloud services.

evidence.text: Gross margin improved compared to the three months ended March 31, 2020, reflecting strong demand for our premium TASER offerings, manufacturing cost improvement, and fixed cost leverage.

Net sales for the Software and Sensors segment increased 34.7% during the three months ended March 31, 2021 as we continued to add users and associated devices to our network. The increase in the aggregate number of users resulted in increased Axon Evidence revenue of $13.1 million. Sales of our Axon Body 3 camera drove most of the $6.9 million increase in Axon Body revenue and the increase of $2.0 million in Axon Dock revenue. The increase in the aggregate number of users and devices also resulted in increased extended warranty revenues of $2.0 million.

factors.summary: Management exhibits mixed confidence, with cautious capital expenditure plans offset by investments in manufacturing and R&D.

evidence.text: Our expectation for capital expenditures of approximately $65 million to $70 million in 2021 remains unchanged.

We are building manufacturing capacity to support our TASER device and cartridge manufacturing lines in response to growing international and federal demand and an increased install base. We are investing in technologies that include our conducted energy devices, body cameras, in-car cameras and other sensors, artificial intelligence, digital evidence management, productivity software, communications software, and technologies that enable real-time situational awareness for public safety.
